evals.name: Basic Live Script Structure
evals.description: Tests that a simple tutorial Live Script has correct structure: sections, text lines, appendix, no banned patterns
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called BasicSignals.m that introduces the reader to generating sine and cosine waves in MATLAB. Include a title, a brief intro paragraph, two sections (one per wave type) each with a plot, and a short summary section. Save the file to the current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses %[text] for text lines
Section breaks include the %[text] ## heading follow-up
assertions.expected: Every %% line on its own is followed within 1-2 lines by a %[text] ## heading line. PASS: %% appears alone on its line, then within 2 lines a %[text] ## Title line appears (intervening blank lines are not allowed by the skill, but code lines between are acceptable). FAIL: a %% break with no %[text] ## heading nearby, or %% with prose only and no markdown heading.

assertions.description: Has required appendix
No figure command in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not contain a standalone 'figure' or 'figure(' call as a MATLAB command. Ignore any mention of the word 'figure' in %[text] prose lines.

assertions.description: No close all in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not contain 'close all' as a MATLAB command on any code line (lines that don't start with %).

assertions.description: No clear command in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not contain 'clear' as a standalone MATLAB command on any code line (lines that don't start with %). The word 'clear' appearing in %[text] prose is acceptable.

assertions.description: The generated Live Script runs without error

evals.name: Escaping Special Characters
evals.description: Tests that the skill correctly escapes underscores, dots after digits, and other special characters in text lines
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called EscapingDemo.m that explains MATLAB variable naming rules. In the text, mention variable names like my_variable, sample_rate, and x_0. Also mention that MATLAB version 9.14 introduced new features. Include a table showing 3 variable naming conventions with examples. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Underscores are escaped in text lines
Some attempt at escaping dots after digits in %[text] lines
assertions.expected: Escaping the period after a digit is a defensive precaution, not strictly required for rendering. NOTE: the Live Editor auto-unescapes single digit-dot sequences (e.g., 9\.14 → 9.14) when re-saving, so an agent who escaped correctly may show no escape in the persisted file. PASS: (a) at least one occurrence of a digit-dot is escaped as \. (e.g., 9\.14), OR (b) the prose contains zero or one digit-dot sequence (single instances are auto-unescaped by Live Editor and cannot reliably be checked from the saved file), OR (c) execution-output evidence shows the agent escaped digit-dots before Live Editor normalization. FAIL: prose contains MULTIPLE distinct digit-dot sequences AND none of them are escaped AND there is no execution-output evidence of agent escaping.

assertions.description: Has a table block
Some attempt at escaping inside table cells
assertions.expected: Escaping inside %[text:table] cells is a defensive precaution. PASS: underscores or dots-after-digits inside the table show at least some escaping (\_ or \.) consistent with how they are escaped in surrounding %[text] lines, OR the table cells contain no characters that need escaping. FAIL only if the table is full of underscores/digit-dots that are clearly unescaped while the surrounding prose escapes them.

evals.name: LaTeX Equations
evals.description: Tests correct LaTeX rendering with proper escaping in equations
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called FourierIntro.m that explains the Discrete Fourier Transform. Include the DFT formula as a display equation in the text, mention that the transform maps N samples to N frequency bins, and show a simple example computing the FFT of a 1024-point signal. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Contains LaTeX equation delimiters
LaTeX uses double backslashes for commands
assertions.expected: LaTeX commands like \sum, \pi, \frac use double backslashes (e.g., \\sum, \\pi, \\frac)

assertions.description: Source does not contain the broken double-backslash + underscore form (\\_) which would render as a literal backslash and break the subscript. Bare _ and single-backslash \_ are both acceptable.
Display equation uses the centered-line wrapper
assertions.expected: At least one centered/display equation appears as a single inline equation wrapped with %[text]{"align":"center"} $ ... $ (i.e., the line begins with %[text]{"align":"center"} and contains a single-$ inline equation). PASS if any line follows this pattern. FAIL if all equations are inline-only with no centered display equation, OR if a $$ ... $$ form appears.

assertions.description: The generated code runs without error

evals.name: Lists and Formatting
evals.description: Tests bullet lists, numbered lists, bold, italic, and the trailing backslash rule
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called WorkflowGuide.m that describes a 5-step data analysis workflow. Use a numbered list for the steps, include bold text for step names, italic for optional notes, and a bulleted list of 3 tips at the end. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses numbered list syntax
Uses bullet list syntax
Last item of each list ends with backslash
assertions.expected: The final item in every bulleted list and every numbered list ends with a trailing backslash (\) before the next non-list line

assertions.description: Uses bold formatting
No blank lines except optionally one before the appendix
assertions.expected: The file contains no blank lines, except for at most a single blank line directly before the %[appendix]{"version":"1.0"} marker. No empty lines anywhere else in the file.

evals.name: No Banned Patterns
evals.description: Tests that the skill avoids mfilename, fprintf, and bare % comments
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called DataLoader.m that loads a CSV file, displays summary statistics, and plots the first column. The script should print the filename being loaded and show the number of rows. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
No mfilename usage in the generated .m file
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not use 'mfilename' as a MATLAB function call on any code line. The skill explicitly forbids it because it returns empty in Live Scripts.

assertions.description: No fprintf; uses disp() or drop-semicolon for output
assertions.expected: The generated .m file does not use 'fprintf' on any code line, AND uses one of the documented alternatives for reader-visible output: either at least one disp() call, or at least one expression statement that intentionally drops the trailing semicolon to display its value. PASS: fprintf absent AND (disp present OR a non-semicolon-terminated output expression present). FAIL: fprintf appears on any code line, OR fprintf is absent but the script uses neither disp() nor drop-semicolon for reader output.

assertions.description: All text lines use %[text] not bare % comments
assertions.expected: Every line of prose/explanatory text uses %[text] syntax, not bare % comment syntax. Code comments (% after code on the same line) are acceptable.

assertions.description: Has the required appendix

evals.name: Anchors and Internal Links
evals.description: Tests that the skill correctly uses anchors and internal hyperlinks to navigate within a Live Script
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called NavDemo.m with three sections (Introduction, Method, Results). At the top, include a brief navigation paragraph with internal links that jump to the Method and Results sections. Add anchors on those section headings so the links work. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses anchor syntax on a heading
Uses internal link syntax
Anchor IDs use only letters, digits, and underscores (no hyphens)
assertions.expected: Every %[text:anchor:ID] in the file uses an ID containing only alphanumeric characters and underscores. No hyphens appear in any anchor ID, because hyphens prevent the anchor from binding.

assertions.description: Internal link IDs match defined anchor IDs
assertions.expected: Every (internal:ID) reference in the file targets an ID that is defined by a corresponding %[text:anchor:ID] elsewhere in the file.

evals.name: No Hard-Wrapping in Prose Paragraphs
evals.description: Tests that multi-sentence prose paragraphs are written on a single %[text] line rather than split across multiple %[text] lines
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called WrapDemo.m that introduces the Fourier transform. Start with a single multi-sentence introductory paragraph (about three sentences) that explains what the Fourier transform does and why it is useful. Then add one section showing a simple fft example on a small synthesized signal. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
The multi-sentence intro paragraph is on a single %[text] line
assertions.expected: The introductory paragraph (the one explaining the Fourier transform) appears on a SINGLE %[text] line containing all of its sentences. PASS: one continuous %[text] line with all ~3 sentences of the intro paragraph. FAIL: the intro paragraph is hard-wrapped across two or more consecutive %[text] lines. Note: a separate %[text] line for a different paragraph (e.g. a section blurb later in the file) is not a violation — only the intro paragraph is in scope.

assertions.description: The generated Live Script runs without error

evals.name: Table of Contents and External Hyperlink
evals.description: Tests that the skill uses the documented Table of Contents marker and external hyperlink syntax
evals.prompt: Create a Live Script called RefDemo.m about MATLAB plotting basics. Include a Table of Contents at the top (under the title). Have at least two sections so the TOC has entries. In the intro paragraph, include a link to the MATLAB documentation at https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script file was created
Uses Table of Contents marker
External hyperlink to the MATLAB docs is present
The generated Live Script runs without error

evals.name: Latex-Heavy Convolution Derivation
evals.description: Tests that the skill produces a Live Script with multiple LaTeX equations rendering correctly when executed and exported. Stresses double-backslash commands, escaped underscores in subscripts, and equation/code/figure interleaving. Verifies via export-to-HTML that equations actually render (not appear as literal $-delimited source).
evals.prompt:
Create a Live Script called ConvolutionTheorem.m that walks through the convolution theorem in two sections: (1) the time-domain convolution definition, written as the integral of f(tau)*g(t-tau) dtau; (2) the Fourier transform identity stating F{f*g} = F(omega) * G(omega). Include intermediate algebraic steps as inline LaTeX equations (with subscripts and the convolution operator). Each section should include one figure: a sample signal in section 1, transform magnitude in section 2. The reader should see rendered equations and embedded figures when they open the document. Save the file to the current directory.

This is an internal evaluation: leave any .html artifact next to the .m when finished — do not delete it.

assertions.description: Live Script source was created
LaTeX commands use double-backslash form in the source
assertions.expected: Inside any $...$ equation in the .m file, LaTeX commands like \\int, \\tau, \\frac, \\omega, \\Delta use double-backslash. PASS: every multi-character LaTeX command inside $...$ is double-escaped. FAIL: any LaTeX command inside $...$ uses a single backslash (e.g., $\int$ instead of $\\int$).

assertions.description: Source does not contain the broken double-backslash + underscore form (\\_ ) which renders as a literal backslash followed by italic-trigger. Note: single-backslash escaped underscore and bare underscore are both acceptable.
Embedded outputs were populated into the appendix
The generated Live Script runs without error
Exported HTML renders equations, figures, and headings correctly
assertions.expected: Open <eval_dir>/ConvolutionTheorem.html. Verify the rendered document: (a) every $...$ block from the .m source is rendered as typeset math — no literal `\frac`, `\int`, `\tau`, `\omega` strings appear as text in the body; (b) section headings render as styled headings, not as literal `## Title`; (c) figures appear inline within their sections; (d) no `%[text]` strings appear in the rendered body. PASS if all four hold. FAIL on any. If ConvolutionTheorem.html is missing, FAIL with reason 'no rendered HTML produced — Verify rendering step likely skipped'.

evals.name: Table of Contents with Cross-References
evals.description: Tests anchor IDs, internal links, and TOC integration. The 'no hyphens in IDs' rule is easy to break. Verifies via export-to-HTML that anchors actually bind and links resolve.
evals.prompt:
Create a Live Script called NumericalMethods.m that documents three root-finding methods: Bisection, Newton's Method, and Secant Method. Include a Table of Contents at the top. Each method gets its own section with an anchor and a brief description. Each section should cross-reference the next method using an internal link (e.g., 'see Newton's Method below'). Add a final summary section that links back to all three methods. Each section should include a small numerical demonstration with one figure showing convergence behavior. The reader should be able to click TOC entries and cross-references and land on the right sections, and see the convergence figures embedded in the document. Save the file to the current directory.

This is an internal evaluation: leave any .html artifact next to the .m when finished — do not delete it.

assertions.description: Live Script source was created
Source uses TOC marker
Source uses anchor syntax
Source uses internal link syntax
Anchor IDs use only letters, digits, underscores
assertions.expected: Every %[text:anchor:ID] in the .m file uses an ID containing only alphanumerics and underscores — no hyphens. PASS: no hyphens in any anchor ID. FAIL: any anchor ID contains a hyphen (the rule explicitly states hyphens prevent the anchor from binding).

assertions.description: Every internal link targets a defined anchor in the same file
assertions.expected: For every (internal:ID) reference in the .m, an anchor with the same ID is defined elsewhere in the file. PASS: every internal link's target ID matches at least one %[text:anchor:ID]. FAIL: any internal link references an ID that is not defined.

assertions.description: The generated Live Script runs without error
Exported HTML renders TOC and cross-references as functional links
assertions.expected: Open <eval_dir>/NumericalMethods.html. Verify the rendered document: (a) the TOC renders as a list of section titles with hyperlinks, not as literal `[text](internal:id)` syntax; (b) internal cross-references in the body render as clickable links, not as literal `[see X](internal:id)` text; (c) anchor markers (`%[text:anchor:id]`) do not leak into the rendered body as visible text. PASS if all three hold. FAIL on any. If NumericalMethods.html is missing, FAIL with reason 'no rendered HTML produced — Verify rendering step likely skipped'.

evals.name: Pathological Escaping
evals.description: Stress test of escape rules. Tests every literal-character case the skill mentions: *, _, [, ], `, \, #, ., ~. Verifies via export-to-HTML that escapes resolved correctly — literals appear as text, not formatting.
evals.prompt:
Create a Live Script called SpecialCharacters.m that documents how to display literal special characters in Live Script body text. The script should have a section for each of these characters: asterisk (*), underscore (_), and backslash (\\). For each character, write a paragraph that uses the literal character in body text. Also include one section where a code comment starts with %% (must NOT be interpreted as a section break) and another section where a code line literally starts with %[text]. Add a final paragraph that uses all three special characters together in one sentence. The reader should see every special character as literal body text, not as formatting. Save the file to the current directory.

This is an internal evaluation: leave any .html artifact next to the .m when finished — do not delete it.

assertions.description: Live Script source was created
Source contains escaped underscore
Source contains escaped asterisk
Code comment containing %% does not unintentionally create a section break
assertions.expected: The agent demonstrates understanding that %% at column 1 of a code line creates a section break. PASS (any of): (a) the code includes '%%\\' (double percent followed by an escape backslash) at column 1, OR (b) the %% appears mid-line in a trailing comment (e.g., 'x = 42; %% comment'), OR (c) the %% is placed inside a string/char literal where it cannot trigger a section break, OR (d) execution-output evidence shows the agent explicitly chose a placement that avoids section-break parsing. FAIL: a bare %% appears at column 1 of a code line in a context the prompt clearly intended as a non-section-break comment.

assertions.description: Code line starting with %[text] is escaped
assertions.expected: If the file contains a code line whose first characters are %[text], it is escaped as %\\[text] to prevent it being parsed as a text region. PASS: the literal %[text] sequence at column 1 of any code line is escaped as %\\[text]. FAIL: a code line begins with literal %[text] without the escape backslash.

assertions.description: The generated Live Script runs without error
Exported HTML shows special characters as literal text and avoids spurious section breaks
assertions.expected: Open <eval_dir>/SpecialCharacters.html. Verify the rendered document: (a) every documented special character (asterisk, underscore, backslash) appears as literal text in the body — not interpreted as bold, italic, or other formatting; (b) the `%%` inside a code comment did NOT trigger an unintended section break — the rendered document has only the section breaks the prompt explicitly asked for; (c) the code line starting with `%[text]` renders as code (or escaped text), not as a Live Editor text region. PASS if all three hold. FAIL on any. If SpecialCharacters.html is missing, FAIL with reason 'no rendered HTML produced — Verify rendering step likely skipped'.

evals.name: No-MATLAB Fallback
evals.description: Tests that the skill produces a clean, valid Live Script source even when MATLAB is not attached and the agent cannot run, embed, or export. The deliverable is a static .m file the user can open and run themselves.
evals.prompt: MATLAB is not attached for this task — generate the file but do not attempt to run, embed outputs, or export it. Create a Live Script called QuickStart.m that introduces a beginner to MATLAB plotting basics. Include a title, a short intro paragraph, and two sections: one plotting a sine wave, one plotting a cosine wave. Save to current directory.

assertions.description: Live Script source was created
Uses %[text] for text lines
Has the required appendix
Source contains no embedded outputs (the agent did not attempt to run/embed)
Source contains no error blocks
Section structure is correct
assertions.expected: Every %% line on its own is followed within 1-2 lines by a %[text] ## heading line. PASS: %% appears alone on its line, then within 2 lines a %[text] ## Title line appears. FAIL: a %% break with no %[text] ## heading nearby.
